rows.description: Dec: 28 to Francis King his Bill
rows.notes: Amounts in the right hand columns are well aligned; pence shows a small looping mark interpreted as '½' (half-penny). Description handwriting slightly cursive but clearly reads 'to Francis King his Bill'. Minor ambiguity in exact date punctuation only.

rows.description: to ye Dean of Ch. Ch. sent for being Venison
rows.notes: Numbers clearly in columns (00 02 06). Description partially abbreviated ('ye Dean of Ch. Ch.') but readable; the word after 'sent for' looks like 'being' or 'bring' but context (Venison) makes 'being' plausible.
Clear short entry and perfectly aligned numeric columns showing 00 01 00. Description 'Corn price' is unambiguous.
Numeric columns clearly read 01 00 00. 'Lighting Lamps' inscription is legible.
Amounts read as 08 13 06 with good column alignment. Surname reads 'Munday' though could be 'Munday/Mundy' — chosen 'Munday' based on letter shapes.

rows.description: 17 to Mr Weston four years Interest due at xmas 1743
rows.notes: Large pounds figure '128' clearly in the pounds column. Description lengthy but legible; 'xmas 1743' reading is clear.

rows.description: to Mr Weston upon bond by common consent
rows.notes: Amount 32.00.00 is well aligned and unambiguous. Description is legible; 'common consent' phrase is clear.
Numeric columns show 00 01 00 clearly. Short description 'Corn price' is clear.

rows.description: to Mr Blop one R[ea]m of Imp. Paper
rows.notes: Amounts read as 02 12 06 clearly. Surname looks like 'Blop' but could be 'Bloop' or 'Blag'; the word before 'of Imp. Paper' appears to be 'one Rham/one Ream' — interpreted as 'one Ream' though handwriting is uncertain.
Numbers (16 05 02) are clear. Surname 'Teasler' is legible though could be 'Teasler/Teasle' — context supports reading as 'Teasler'.
Amounts 13 17 00 are clear. Description reads 'to ye Cook for Gardins' (spelling archaic); 'Gardins' could be 'Gardies' but intent is clear.
Numeric columns show 00 08 06 clearly. 'Mrs Heritage' is legible.
Amount 41 08 04 is clear with proper column alignment. 'Mr Cozens' reading is straightforward.

rows.description: May 19 to wine to entertain Mr Wilmot in Ch.
rows.notes: Amount 00 01 06 clearly visible. The description has minor abbreviations ('in Ch.'), but 'entertain Mr Wilmot' is legible.

rows.description: June 12 to Mr Boyles Works for ye Library
rows.notes: Amount 05 05 00 is clear. 'Mr Boyles Works for ye Library' reads well; 'Boyles' could be 'Boyle's' but the text is legible.

rows.description: July 7 to Mrs Combes her Bill
rows.notes: Amount 02 04 00 clear. Name 'Mrs Combes' and phrase 'her Bill' are legible.

rows.description: 10 to Mr Will. Talbot his Plate money
rows.notes: Amount 06 00 00 clear and perfectly aligned. 'Mr Will. Talbot' readable; 'Plate money' clear.

rows.description: Aug 4 to Mr Townsend his Bill
rows.notes: Numbers read 44 05 11 with a small trailing mark interpreted as '3q' (three farthings) = 0.75. 'Mr Townsend' wording is clear.

rows.description: 10 to Landtax for ye year 1742. & 1743. & Aid
rows.notes: Amount 14 08 00 clear. Description long but legible; interpreted as land tax for the years noted. Minor punctuation uncertain.

rows.description: 20 brought to acct w't was pd Newman for wine to entertain Coll. Ten't
rows.notes: Amount 00 03 00 clear. Description spans two lines and is slightly cramped; read as 'brought to acct w't was pd Newman for wine to entertain Coll. Ten't' — some abbreviations expanded by context.
Straightforward repeat of 'Corn price' with clear numbers 00 01 00.

rows.description: 25 to Salisbury Carrier for 2 Boxes from Mr Crabb
rows.notes: Amount 00 09 04 clear. 'Salisbury Carrier for 2 Boxes from Mr Crabb' readable though 'Crabb' could be alternate spelling; chosen as appears.

rows.description: Oct. 10 to Mr Horner for Paper Ink &c.
rows.notes: Numeric 00 17 04 clearly aligned. 'Mr Horner for Paper Ink &c.' is legible.

rows.description: to Edgley for binding two Buttry Books
rows.notes: Amount 01 04 00 clear. Surname reads as 'Edgley'; the item 'Buttry Books' (buttery books) is legible though archaic spelling may vary.
Amount 00 15 00 clear. Short description 'to Wine Oct. 5' is unambiguous.

rows.description: 20 to Bayliffe for carrying away Rubbish
rows.notes: Amount 02 03 00 clearly aligned. 'Bayliffe for carrying away Rubbish' is legible.
Stacked subtotal column. Pounds (386), shillings (12), pence (06) are crisply aligned; small final mark interpreted as a farthing (¼) = 0.25. Column alignment used to determine boundaries.
Stacked subtotal with clear numerals 179.16.02; no fraction mark visible. Vertical alignment unambiguous.
Stacked subtotal showing 90 07 05 with a clear half-penny loop interpreted as ½ = 0.5. Column rulings (implied by alignment) confirm correct column splits.
Stacked subtotal with numbers 94.05.05 and a distinct '3q' style mark interpreted as three farthings (¾ = 0.75). Alignment and digits are clear.
Boxed/boxed-looking total on right reading £751.01.08 3/4. Numerals and fractional 3/4 are distinct and centered with a surrounding box, making this a clear final sum-on-folio line.

Text is abbreviated/Latinised ('Re Soluti quæ erant') and handwriting is tighter here; the numeric repetition of £751.01.08 3/4 matches the boxed total above. Slight uncertainty in exact Latin spelling, but numbers match boxed total.
Phrase 'Deductis receptis quæ erant' (deductions received) is partially abbreviated but understandable. Numerals 739.02.09 readable though ink slightly lighter than above totals.

Final balancing line shows approx. £11.18.09 with a small trailing fraction interpreted as 3/4 (0.75). Handwriting in this line is cramped and there is some overwriting/scratching making the pence and fraction less certain, but the alignment and remaining numerals support this reading.
Signature/attestation line. Name reads as 'Jacobus Dogcumbe' (possible variant spellings); no amounts present. Handwritten signature-style script reduces confidence.
Signature line 'Francis Webber Jun. Rector' visible but written in cursive; no associated amounts. 'Jun.' interpreted as junior.
Signature-style name; letters after name appear to be degree/office abbreviations 'Th. B.' or similar. Ink and flourish lower legibility.
Signature 'Car: Webber A.M.' (abbreviated Christian name 'Car:' for 'Car:') readable but in cursive; interpreted as a header/signature line with no amounts.
Another signature line; reads as 'Geo: Keppel M.A.' though letters are stylised. No amounts. Moderate uncertainty due to script flourishes.
Final signature-like line reads 'Tho: Bray' with trailing abbreviation that could be 'Th. B.' or similar. No amounts present; handwriting is faint in places.
